name: lockfile

metadata.name: lockfile
classifiers: Operating System :: Microsoft :: Windows :: Windows NT/2000
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
Note: This package is **deprecated**. It is highly preferred that instead of
using this code base that instead [fasteners] or [oslo.concurrency] is
used instead. For any questions or comments or further help needed
please email [openstack-dev] and prefix your email subject
with `[oslo][pylockfile]` (for a faster response).

The lockfile package exports a LockFile class which provides a simple API for
locking files.  Unlike the Windows msvcrt.locking function, the fcntl.lockf
and flock functions, and the deprecated posixfile module, the API is
identical across both Unix (including Linux and Mac) and Windows platforms.
The lock mechanism relies on the atomic nature of the link (on Unix) and
mkdir (on Windows) system calls.  An implementation based on SQLite is also
provided, more as a demonstration of the possibilities it provides than as
production-quality code.

Note: In version 0.9 the API changed in two significant ways:

> - It changed from a module defining several classes to a package containing
>   several modules, each defining a single class.
> - Where classes had been named SomethingFileLock before the last two words
>   have been reversed, so that class is now SomethingLockFile.

The previous module-level definitions of LinkFileLock, MkdirFileLock and
SQLiteFileLock will be retained until the 1.0 release.

To install:

> python setup.py install

- Documentation: <http://docs.openstack.org/developer/pylockfile>
- Source: <http://git.openstack.org/cgit/openstack/pylockfile>
- Bugs: <http://bugs.launchpad.net/pylockfile>

[fasteners]: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/fasteners
[openstack-dev]: http://lists.openstack.org/cgi-bin/mailman/listinfo/openstack-dev
[oslo.concurrency]: http://docs.openstack.org/developer/oslo.concurrency/

classes.name: lockfile.linklockfile.LinkLockFile
documentation:
Lock access to a file using atomic property of link(2).

>>> lock = LinkLockFile('somefile')
>>> lock = LinkLockFile('somefile', threaded=False)

documentation: Lock access to a file using symlink(2).

functions.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.read_pid_from_pidfile
documentation:
Read the PID recorded in the named PID file.

Read and return the numeric PID recorded as text in the named
PID file. If the PID file cannot be read, or if the content is
not a valid PID, return `None`.

functions.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.write_pid_to_pidfile
documentation:
Write the PID in the named PID file.

Get the numeric process ID (“PID”) of the current process
and write it to the named file as a line of text.

functions.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.remove_existing_pidfile
documentation:
Remove the named PID file if it exists.

Removing a PID file that doesn't already exist puts us in the
desired state, so we ignore the condition if the file does not
exist.

classes.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile

methods.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile.read_pid
documentation: Get the PID from the lock file.

methods.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile.is_locked
documentation:
Test if the lock is currently held.

The lock is held if the PID file for this lock exists.

methods.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile.i_am_locking
documentation:
Test if the lock is held by the current process.

Returns `True` if the current process ID matches the
number stored in the PID file.

methods.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile.acquire
documentation:
Acquire the lock.

Creates the PID file for this lock, or raises an error if
the lock could not be acquired.

methods.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile.release
documentation:
Release the lock.

Removes the PID file to release the lock, or raises an
error if the current process does not hold the lock.

methods.name: lockfile.pidlockfile.PIDLockFile.break_lock
documentation:
Break an existing lock.

Removes the PID file if it already exists, otherwise does
nothing.

Lockfile implemented as a Unix PID file.

The lock file is a normal file named by the attribute `path`.
A lock's PID file contains a single line of text, containing
the process ID (PID) of the process that acquired the lock.

>>> lock = PIDLockFile('somefile')
>>> lock = PIDLockFile('somefile')

methods.name: lockfile.sqlitelockfile.SQLiteLockFile.__init__
documentation:
>>> lock = SQLiteLockFile('somefile')
>>> lock = SQLiteLockFile('somefile', threaded=False)

modules.name: lockfile
documentation:
lockfile.py - Platform-independent advisory file locks.

Requires Python 2.5 unless you apply 2.4.diff
Locking is done on a per-thread basis instead of a per-process basis.

Usage:

>>> lock = LockFile('somefile')
>>> try:
...     lock.acquire()
... except AlreadyLocked:
...     print 'somefile', 'is locked already.'
... except LockFailed:
...     print 'somefile', 'can\'t be locked.'
... else:
...     print 'got lock'
got lock
>>> print lock.is_locked()
True
>>> lock.release()

>>> lock = LockFile('somefile')
>>> print lock.is_locked()
False
>>> with lock:
...    print lock.is_locked()
True
>>> print lock.is_locked()
False

>>> lock = LockFile('somefile')
>>> # It is okay to lock twice from the same thread...
>>> with lock:
...     lock.acquire()
...
>>> # Though no counter is kept, so you can't unlock multiple times...
>>> print lock.is_locked()
False

Exceptions:

    Error - base class for other exceptions
        LockError - base class for all locking exceptions
            AlreadyLocked - Another thread or process already holds the lock
            LockFailed - Lock failed for some other reason
        UnlockError - base class for all unlocking exceptions
            AlreadyUnlocked - File was not locked.
            NotMyLock - File was locked but not by the current thread/process
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: lockfile.LinkFileLock
documentation:
Factory function provided for backwards compatibility.

Do not use in new code.  Instead, import LinkLockFile from the
lockfile.linklockfile module.

functions.name: lockfile.MkdirFileLock
documentation:
Factory function provided for backwards compatibility.

Do not use in new code.  Instead, import MkdirLockFile from the
lockfile.mkdirlockfile module.

functions.name: lockfile.SQLiteFileLock
documentation:
Factory function provided for backwards compatibility.

Do not use in new code.  Instead, import SQLiteLockFile from the
lockfile.mkdirlockfile module.

functions.name: lockfile.locked
documentation:
Decorator which enables locks for decorated function.

Arguments:
 - path: path for lockfile.
 - timeout (optional): Timeout for acquiring lock.

 Usage:
     @locked('/var/run/myname', timeout=0)
     def myname(...):
         ...

classes.name: lockfile.Error
documentation:
Base class for other exceptions.

>>> try:
...   raise Error
... except Exception:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.LockError
documentation:
Base class for error arising from attempts to acquire the lock.

>>> try:
...   raise LockError
... except Error:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.LockTimeout
documentation:
Raised when lock creation fails within a user-defined period of time.

>>> try:
...   raise LockTimeout
... except LockError:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.AlreadyLocked
documentation:
Some other thread/process is locking the file.

>>> try:
...   raise AlreadyLocked
... except LockError:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.LockFailed
documentation:
Lock file creation failed for some other reason.

>>> try:
...   raise LockFailed
... except LockError:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.UnlockError
documentation:
Base class for errors arising from attempts to release the lock.

>>> try:
...   raise UnlockError
... except Error:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.NotLocked
documentation:
Raised when an attempt is made to unlock an unlocked file.

>>> try:
...   raise NotLocked
... except UnlockError:
...   pass

classes.name: lockfile.NotMyLock
documentation:
Raised when an attempt is made to unlock a file someone else locked.

>>> try:
...   raise NotMyLock
... except UnlockError:
...   pass

methods.name: lockfile._SharedBase.acquire
documentation:
Acquire the lock.

* If timeout is omitted (or None), wait forever trying to lock the
  file.

* If timeout > 0, try to acquire the lock for that many seconds.  If
  the lock period expires and the file is still locked, raise
  LockTimeout.

* If timeout <= 0, raise AlreadyLocked immediately if the file is
  already locked.

methods.name: lockfile._SharedBase.release
documentation:
Release the lock.

If the file is not locked, raise NotLocked.

methods.name: lockfile._SharedBase.__enter__
documentation: Context manager support.

methods.name: lockfile._SharedBase.__exit__
documentation: Context manager support.

methods.name: lockfile.LockBase.is_locked
documentation: Tell whether or not the file is locked.

methods.name: lockfile.LockBase.i_am_locking
documentation: Return True if this object is locking the file.

methods.name: lockfile.LockBase.break_lock
documentation: Remove a lock.  Useful if a locking thread failed to unlock.
Base class for platform-specific lock classes.
